model_findings.description: The new `--host` option accepts arbitrary bind addresses and `uvicorn.run` uses it directly, so `ab serve --openai --host 0.0.0.0` exposes an unauthenticated endpoint that can execute local Codex/Gemini/Claude/Hermes CLIs with the user's credentials and environment. The docs describe this as localhost-only tooling, but the code has no explicit opt-in guard such as `--allow-remote` or host validation.

The chat completions endpoint accepts unbounded `messages` content and synchronously starts a backend subprocess per request, with each process allowed to run for up to 120 seconds. A local or accidentally exposed client can submit very large prompts or many concurrent requests and consume memory, CPU, process slots, and paid/model CLI quota; there is no request body limit, prompt length cap, or concurrency gate at the proxy boundary.

Because the request model allows extra OpenAI fields but the endpoint ignores them, a client sending `stream: true` receives a normal JSON completion instead of an event-stream response or a clear unsupported-parameter error. Many OpenAI-compatible clients switch parsing behavior based on `stream`, so this will fail at runtime in a way that looks like a protocol bug rather than an intentional Phase 1 limitation.
